title: Schwinger-Keldysh Path Integral for Gauge theories
authors: Greg Kaplanek, Maria Mylova, Andrew J. Tolley
abstract: We develop the Schwinger-Keldysh path-integral formalism for open non-Abelian gauge theories that are gauge-fixed via the BRST method in covariant gauges. We focus on generic initial states, pure and mixed, specified at finite times suitable for non-equilibrium processes. We pay particular attention to the handling of the indefinite Hilbert space, the construction of BRST-invariant Schrodinger picture wavefunctionals, density matrices and inner product, the implementation of the Hata-Kugo prescription, and the role of boundary terms at both the initial and final times. We highlight the advantages of the Nakanishi-Lautrup field representation in dealing with initial/final conditions. The resulting Schwinger-Keldysh path integral is manifestly invariant under a diagonal (retarded) BRST symmetry for arbitrary physical initial states, whether pure or mixed. From this, we obtain the corresponding Ward-Takahashi-Slavnov-Taylor identities, valid perturbatively. Non-perturbatively the Gribov ambiguity is expected to break or modify the BRST symmetry. The naive advanced BRST symmetry is shown to be explicitly violated by the in-in boundary conditions. We show that the Feynman-Vernon influence functional derived by integrating out charged matter and/or hard gluon modes remains (perturbatively) BRST invariant. When the Open EFT action is expanded to second order in advanced fields it exhibits an exact symmetry under a contraction of the original BRST symmetry. This Keldysh BRST symmetry is equivalent to the BRST associated with the retarded gauge transformations together with a linearly realized BRST transformation of the advanced fields. These govern the structure of the leading terms in an Open EFT. We illustrate this with the explicit example of Hard Thermal Loop Effective Theory, and construct the general form of the Open EFT in a Higgs phase when all gauge symmetries are spontaneously broken.
summary: This paper provides a rigorous mathematical framework for studying non-equilibrium gauge theories using the Schwinger-Keldysh formalism. It solves the problem of maintaining BRST gauge invariance when dealing with arbitrary initial states and boundary terms at finite times. By using the Nakanishi-Lautrup representation, the authors show how to construct consistent effective field theories for open systems. This work is foundational for understanding the real-time dynamics of plasmas and symmetry-breaking phases in high-energy physics.
main_problem: Developing a consistent BRST-invariant Schwinger-Keldysh path-integral formalism for non-equilibrium, non-Abelian gauge theories with generic initial states.
main_result: The authors derive a path integral that is manifestly invariant under a diagonal (retarded) BRST symmetry and establish the framework for constructing BRST-invariant Open Effective Field Theories (EFTs).
method: The study utilizes the Schwinger-Keldysh (closed time path) formalism, Nakanishi-Lautrup field representation, and the Hata-Kugo prescription for handling indefinite Hilbert spaces and boundary conditions.
model_or_system: Non-Abelian Yang-Mills theories coupled to charged matter, specifically focusing on the Hard Thermal Loop (HTL) effective theory and gauge theories in a Higgs phase.
key_observables: Real-time expectation values, in-in correlation functions, Wigner functions, and Ward-Takahashi-Slavnov-Taylor identities.
important_parameters: Non-equilibrium regimes, finite-time boundaries, gauge-fixing parameter (xi), and coupling constant (g).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes spatial boundary contributions can be neglected and focuses on the perturbative regime, noting that the Gribov ambiguity may break BRST symmetry non-perturbatively.
paper_structure: The paper begins with the quantization of the indefinite Hilbert space and the construction of BRST-invariant wavefunctions, moves to the development of the Schwinger-Keldysh path integral for non-Abelian theories, and concludes with the application to Open EFTs like HTL and the Higgs phase.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
